00f6a3076bed5c035b341a3f32fe31bbec35503c.reason: The cookie value flows only into the environment variable array (`argsEnv`, line 72) passed as the second argument to `Runtime.exec(String[], String[])`. The command array (`args`, line 71) contains only the hardcoded path returned by `getInsecureOSCommandString()` (lines 68–70, which resolves a fixed script from the classpath — never user-controllable). The array overload of `Runtime.exec` does not invoke a shell, so environment-variable values are never interpreted as commands or shell metacharacters. There is no command injection path: the tainted data reaches the environment block of the child process, not the command line.

014b0ed5775851bd7ded0eda04909d952a6b0f6d.reason: Attacker-controlled HTTP request parameter 'BenchmarkTest01360' flows through getParameterMap() -> param -> bar -> r.exec(cmd + bar) with zero sanitization, validation, or escaping. The inner class Test.doSomething() deterministically returns param (switch on 'C'). On Windows, cmd.exe /c interprets the concatenated string as a shell command line, allowing injection via &, |, etc. On non-Windows, cmd is empty so the user input runs as the executable name directly.
